title: alopecurus myosuroides subsp. tonsus
intro: Alopecurus myosuroides subsp. tonsus, commonly known as the black-grass, is a subspecies of grassy weed native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful in treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Alopecurus myosuroides subsp. tonsus include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Traditional herbal preparations of this plant include teas and infusions, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
